groups.instruction: Answer the questions based on the audio.
groups.prompt_html:
MULTIPLE CHOICE 51

Questions 31 - 35

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

31 A passive building is one which …

A ... is designed to reduce the need for energy for warmth and cooling.
B ... includes elements which can create energy, for example solar power.
C ... is designed and built using as little energy as possible.

32 Buildings that use passive design features are likely to …

A ... be less secure than conventionally designed houses.
B ... become increasingly popular in years to come.
C ... reduce a building’s running costs by 40 per cent.

33 The lecturer believes that in future there will be more demand for…

A ... housing built using low energy techniques and materials.
B ... cheaper housing which creates high energy bills.
C ... more expensive housing which has low energy bills.

34 An example of psychological thermal discomfort is …

A ... feeling too hot when outdoors on a warm, sunny day.
B ... feeling too cold when sitting beside a window in a warm room.
C ... feeling too cold in an inadequately heated room.

35 According to the lecturer, design software is …

A ... only effective at producing physically comfortable buildings.
B ... only effective at producing psychologically comfortable buildings.
C ... effective at producing both physically and psychologically comfortable buildings.

items.prompt_html: A passive building is one which …
Buildings that use passive design features are likely to …
The lecturer believes that in future there will be more demand for…
An example of psychological thermal discomfort is …
According to the lecturer, design software is …

options.label: A
options.text: ... only effective at producing physically comfortable buildings.

options.label: B
options.text: ... only effective at producing psychologically comfortable buildings.

options.label: C
options.text: ... effective at producing both physically and psychologically comfortable buildings.
